text.title: ddd-value-objects Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for ddd-value-objects. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Value Objects MUST be immutable. In Python use @dataclass(frozen=True). In Java/Kotlin use records or final fields with no setters. In C# use readonly structs or record types. Mutation must be impossible by the type system, not by convention.
rule.rationale: Mutable Value Objects create aliasing bugs: two callers share the same instance and one mutates it, silently affecting the other. Immutability makes Value Objects safe to pass, store, and compare by value.

rule.statement: Value Objects MUST validate their invariants in __post_init__ (Python) or the constructor (Java/C#/TS). An invalid Value Object MUST NOT be constructible — it must raise/throw immediately.
rule.rationale: Deferred validation (checking after construction) allows invalid objects to propagate through the system. Fail-fast construction makes illegal states unrepresentable.

rule.statement: Value Object operations that produce a new value MUST return a new instance, never mutate the existing one. Example: Money.add(other) returns Money(self.amount + other.amount, self.currency).
rule.rationale: Mutation methods on a supposedly immutable object are a trap: frozen dataclasses prevent this in Python, but in Java/C# the invariant must be enforced by code review or tests.
